per_case.golden_answer: DIFC Law No. 6 of 2013 is the Arbitration Law of 2008 Amendment Law, and DIFC Law No. 3 of 2013 is the General Partnership Law 2004 Amendment Law.

per_case.golden_answer: The interpretation sections of the Operating Law 2018, Trust Law 2018, and Common Reporting Standard Law 2018 all contain interpretative provisions and defined terms explaining how the law should be read and applied.
per_case.our_answer: Section 1(1) of the interpretation provisions in both the Operating Law 2018 and the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018 provides that: (a) a reference to a statutory provision includes amendments or re-enactments; (b) “person” includes natural persons, bodies corporate or unincorporate, partnerships, unincorporated associations, governments, or states; (c) obligations to publish documents include both printed and electronic forms unless otherwise stated; and (d) “day” means a calendar day, with obligations falling on weekends or public holidays deferred to the next working day.

per_case.golden_answer: Schedule 1 of both the Operating Law 2018 and the Trust Law 2018 contains interpretative provisions and defined terms used in the law.
per_case.our_answer:
1. Schedule 1 contains interpretative provisions which apply to the Law. (cite: bee43bdc6ca06c2a04f4126d4b94fa4be3d47a62e8b3bdd8d0ddce986dff25a6:3:0:d95a8fab, 4e387152960c1029b3711cacb05b287b13c977bc61f2558059a62b7b427a62eb:4:anchor:1d8180ca)
2. Schedule 1 contains a list of defined terms used in the Law. (cite: bee43bdc6ca06c2a04f4126d4b94fa4be3d47a62e8b3bdd8d0ddce986dff25a6:3:0:d95a8fab, 4e387152960c1029b3711cacb05b287b13c977bc61f2558059a62b7b427a62eb:4:anchor:1d8180ca)

per_case.golden_answer: The Foundations Law 2018 and the Trust Law 2018 are the 2018 laws in the corpus whose schedules include provisions that refer to arbitration-related matters.

The 2018 laws in the corpus made by the Ruler of Dubai include the Companies Law 2018, the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018, the Foundations Law 2018, the Operating Law 2018, the Real Property Law 2018, and the Trust Law 2018.
per_case.our_answer:
1. in the form now attached the DIFC Laws Amendment Law 2018 (cite: fe81efddb24f01f4455fc6b16fc0867615e840b0d36e7abfd1441e5c2eaed92d:1:anchor:1e2b6cab)
2. in the form now attached the Strata Title Law Amendment Law 2018 (cite: f513711cc3a06447b41a1b798acb1fae36a82ea4d95a0aa3977d4a71ea79d704:1:anchor:3327c4a0)
3. in the form now attached the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018 (cite: d1a939b689b84a9dd683e3dfc93ccb238e310476f9c3249492cf09868852ebd9:1:anchor:3a89968b)

per_case.golden_answer: The Dematerialised Investments Regulations cite the Companies Law and the Personal Property Law as conferring powers for the regulations dealing with uncertificated investments and company structure.
Data Protection Law 2020 comes into force on 1 July 2020; Employment Law 2019 comes into force ninety (90) days following the date specified in the Enactment Notice.
per_case.our_answer:
1. consolidated version (July 2025) of the DIFC Data Protection Law: Comes into force on 1 July 2020 (cite: a3c77d9b52fbdca1709099a06596ca14c68fedb02b94a97cdff4f121325a740a:3:1:5acfb226, a3c77d9b52fbdca1709099a06596ca14c68fedb02b94a97cdff4f121325a740a:4:anchor:33e5c76e, a3c77d9b52fbdca1709099a06596ca14c68fedb02b94a97cdff4f121325a740a:4:anchor:9bb52685)
2. Employment Law 2019: The provided sources do not contain its commencement provision.

per_case.golden_answer: Architeriors Interior Design (L.L.C)
Law No. 16 of 2011 superseded Articles 2, 4, 5 and 7 of Law No. 12 of 2004; the superseded Article 4 dealt with the duties and powers of the Chief Justice of the Courts.
per_case.our_answer: Articles 2, 4, 5, and 7 of Law No. 12 of 2004 are explicitly superseded by Law No. 16 of 2011, as stated in Article 1 of the amending law (cite: be59024d9c82dbc4a8b716e94bd1169387066998e415fb598e677eb9ad104c29:0:1:ff56313c). Article 4 of Law No. 12 of 2004, which was superseded, primarily addressed the duties and powers of the Chief Justice of the Courts, including general supervision and specific administrative functions (cite: 2367e45430240e0210f1c54962e7e3a96b6b0908c56e01eb77ced99e114d61be:1:2:f2ce4b20).

per_case.golden_answer: The Leasing Regulations prescribe a penalty of USD 10,000 for using leased premises for an illegal purpose. No equally clear offence penalty for the Strata Title Law was identified in the reviewed Strata Title Regulations pages.
per_case.our_answer:
1. Strata Title Law DIFC Law: The penalty is 1,000 USD (cite: b486db089615010a9b45bc71411b5a238a0a0b9aaf074d875f98f5b934f75079:2:0:d2d7ab70)
2. Leasing Regulations: The penalty is 10,000 USD (cite: 02ec30ff6c98fa3adccd3834d73d76a2ae11dab91f2b4ea3501ff24db27436cc:4:0:8ba2168f)

per_case.golden_answer: Both the DIFC Laws Amendment Law No. 3 of 2024 and the Law of Security Law No. 4 of 2024 come into force on the fifth business day after enactment.
per_case.our_answer:
1. Personal Property Law 2005: Comes into force on the date specified in the Enactment Notice in respect of this Law (cite: c94f011cc2ece6e5f8f45479a21a1ef18764e6616ac0d50ab9055e2a99383af9:2:0:cbb4502d)
2. the Law of Security Law 2024: Shall come into force on the 5th business day after enactment (not counting the day of enactment for this purpose) (cite: 77ed6765ad5556f347f1e9fdfb0ce72dba0451713359f73aae3fbc00b35ede32:0:0:da4023eb)

per_case.golden_answer: The Limited Liability Partnership Law and the Non Profit Incorporated Organisations Law are both administered by the Registrar.
per_case.our_answer:
1. Limited Liability Partnership Law 2004: This Law and any legislation made for the purpose of this Law is administered by the Registrar (cite: b82ac8228e051d96bf8d706d3251893ebff1c9457b066fce3b7cb99af956f2a7:3:0:b78b1c5c)
2. Non Profit Incorporated Organisations Law 2012: This Law and any legislation made for the purposes of this Law are administered by the Registrar (cite: 2071fcaf840c53c30a7f4851ca1c0fc1a33475370170e8c22785f9e92d26608a:2:0:ff97aeeb)

per_case.golden_answer: Pre-existing accounts: 2016-12-31; new accounts: 2017-01-01; enactment date: 2018-03-14.
per_case.our_answer:
1. Pre-existing Accounts: The effective date is 31 December, 2016 (cite: 607d58415d7bd7eb893678ef2bbbda6b4793c665efa5e3fd42ec206cf64a746a:3:anchor:56a0fcd7)
2. New Accounts: The effective date is 1 January, 2017 (cite: 607d58415d7bd7eb893678ef2bbbda6b4793c665efa5e3fd42ec206cf64a746a:3:anchor:56a0fcd7)
3. Common Reporting Standard Law 2018: The date of enactment is 14th day of March 2018 (cite: d1a939b689b84a9dd683e3dfc93ccb238e310476f9c3249492cf09868852ebd9:0:0:d16f0918)

per_case.golden_answer: The Court dismissed the application for a default costs certificate and ordered the Claimant to bear its own costs of the application.
per_case.our_answer: The Application is dismissed (cite: 443e04bc1a78940b3fcd5438d24b6c5f182a276d354a3108e738b193675de032:0:0:7e3612a4). The Claimant shall bear its own costs of the Application (cite: 443e04bc1a78940b3fcd5438d24b6c5f182a276d354a3108e738b193675de032:0:0:7e3612a4).

per_case.golden_answer: Law No. 1 of 2025 broadens Article 6(3)(b) so the Data Protection Law also applies to processing of Personal Data in the DIFC by a Controller or Processor, including Sub-processors, where the processing forms part of stable arrangements and is not merely occasional.
per_case.our_answer: Article 6(3) of the Data Protection Law 2020, as amended by Law No. 1 of 2025, clarifies that the law applies to processing of Personal Data in the DIFC by a Controller or Processor (or their Sub-processors), regardless of incorporation, as part of stable arrangements and not on an occasional basis, and defines “in the DIFC” as when the means or personnel used are physically located in the DIFC (cite: ef59bbc482cdca0268be35906d6274916740cb92a17c5d5d914b0f09807154f7:3:0:2ae7fd31). This amendment refines the scope of application to focus on stable, non-occasional processing activities physically conducted in the DIFC, including data transfers out of the DIFC (cite: ef59bbc482cdca0268be35906d6274916740cb92a17c5d5d914b0f09807154f7:3:0:2ae7fd31).

per_case.golden_answer: Fursa Consulting
Onora

The Board of Directors of the DIFCA has authority to make the Dematerialised Investments Regulations, under powers conferred by Article 116 of the Companies Law and Article 43 of the Personal Property Law.
per_case.our_answer: The Board of Directors of the DIFCA has the authority to make the Dematerialised Investments Regulations (DIR) under Article 116 of the Companies Law and Article 43 of the Personal Property Law. These laws are explicitly cited as conferring the powers for these regulations (page 3, DIR Introduction) (cite: 20be16a68c4d24768f569bcde5ddb29437204006c0c4262296c350b365672cd1:2:0:62069597).

per_case.golden_answer: DIFC Law No. 5 of 2018 is the Companies Law 2018 and DIFC Law No. 4 of 2019 is the Intellectual Property Law 2019; both consolidated versions were last updated in March 2022.
per_case.our_answer:
1. Law No. 5 of 2018 - Title: Companies Law 2018 - Last updated (consolidated version): March 2022 (cite: efa6f9ce52c6a8f3f2725d44a7c328211ad9ad1dd2fdb7ff0f0dd1005369a91a:0:0:934a1702)
2. Law No. 4 of 2019 - Title: Intellectual Property Law 2019 - Last updated (consolidated version): March 2022 (cite: 7d2514bd549b3771c085b0d0b74c6e4353feb3895811046061142738ccfc6869:0:0:d955e680)

per_case.golden_answer: The DIFC Laws Amendment Law, DIFC Law No. 8 of 2018, was enacted on 2018-11-05 and amended multiple DIFC laws.
per_case.our_answer: The Enactment Notice provides that the DIFC Laws Amendment Law, DIFC Law No. 8 of 2018, was enacted on 5 November 2018 and came into force on the 5th business day after enactment. This law amended both the Limited Partnership Law and the Trust Law, as indicated in their respective amendment histories (cite: fe81efddb24f01f4455fc6b16fc0867615e840b0d36e7abfd1441e5c2eaed92d:0:0:e4bd25d2).

per_case.golden_answer: The Leasing Law 2020 and the Trust Law 2018 are both administered by the DIFCA.
per_case.our_answer:
1. Leasing Law, DIFC Law No. 1 of 2020: DIFCA (cite: 8c6d34f8833e88c664c99576d875f0d1bcab6bd6360e9c6fe6dec3f50f9bde01:3:1:98026e07)
2. Trust Law 2018: DIFCA (cite: 9ad89c8f1ca0b257e77e8bbc354e7aff4ec6bcce10d28f172b71e7918908c221:4:0:0d64b5da)
